rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its ports in AGREE contracts
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by respecting AADL scope rules
Use only identifiers that are visible in the current scope
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when inserting annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for bindings and implementation-specific contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not reference implementation-only elements in component type contracts
Do not insert annex blocks outside of valid component or implementation scopes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
